questions.text: Choose the word that best fits the blank: The true intent of the policy remained _____ beneath layers of bureaucratic language.
questions.solution:
Question word: **Obtect** (the correct fit)

**English Meaning:** Covered or concealed; hidden under a layer.
**Hindi Meaning:** ढका हुआ, छिपा हुआ
**Synonyms:** Concealed, hidden, covered, shrouded
**Antonyms:** Exposed, visible, revealed
**Example:** The true intent of the policy remained obtect beneath layers of bureaucratic language.

**Option 0: exposed**
**English Meaning:** Not covered or hidden; open to view.
**Hindi Meaning:** उजागर, प्रकट
**Synonyms:** Visible, revealed
**Antonyms:** Obtect
**Example:** The hidden files were exposed during the audit.

**Option 1: visible**
**English Meaning:** Able to be seen clearly.
**Hindi Meaning:** दृश्यमान
**Synonyms:** Revealed, apparent
**Antonyms:** Obtect
**Example:** The mistake became visible to everyone.

**Option 2: obtect** (Correct)
**English Meaning:** Covered or concealed; hidden under a layer.
**Hindi Meaning:** ढका हुआ, छिपा हुआ
**Synonyms:** Concealed, hidden, covered
**Antonyms:** Exposed, visible
**Example:** The true intent of the policy remained obtect beneath layers of bureaucratic language.

**Option 3: revealed**
**English Meaning:** Made known or shown.
**Hindi Meaning:** प्रकट किया गया
**Synonyms:** Exposed, visible
**Antonyms:** Obtect
**Example:** The secret was revealed in the meeting.

**Option 4: apparent**
**English Meaning:** Clearly visible or obvious.
**Hindi Meaning:** स्पष्ट
**Synonyms:** Visible, revealed
**Antonyms:** Obtect
**Example:** The error was apparent to all experts.

Only 'obtect' correctly conveys something deliberately hidden.

questions.text: Choose the closest synonym for 'Peregrinate':
questions.solution:
Question word: **Peregrinate** (the correct synonym is 'wander extensively')

**English Meaning:** To travel or wander extensively from place to place.
**Hindi Meaning:** भ्रमण करना, इधर-उधर घूमना
**Synonyms:** Wander, roam, travel, journey
**Antonyms:** Settle, remain, stay
**Example:** Scholars often peregrinate across institutions to broaden their academic perspectives.

**Option 0: settle permanently**
**English Meaning:** To establish a fixed home.
**Hindi Meaning:** स्थायी रूप से बसना
**Synonyms:** Reside steadily
**Antonyms:** Peregrinate
**Example:** Many families settle permanently in cities.

**Option 1: remain fixed**
**English Meaning:** To stay in one place without moving.
**Hindi Meaning:** स्थिर रहना
**Synonyms:** Stay rooted
**Antonyms:** Peregrinate
**Example:** The old tree remained fixed for decades.

**Option 2: wander extensively** (Correct)
**English Meaning:** To travel or wander extensively from place to place.
**Hindi Meaning:** भ्रमण करना, इधर-उधर घूमना
**Synonyms:** Roam, journey
**Antonyms:** Settle, remain
**Example:** Scholars often peregrinate across institutions to broaden their academic perspectives.

**Option 3: stay rooted**
**English Meaning:** To remain firmly in one location.
**Hindi Meaning:** जड़ें जमाए रखना
**Synonyms:** Remain fixed
**Antonyms:** Peregrinate
**Example:** Traditional families stay rooted in villages.

**Option 4: reside steadily**
**English Meaning:** To live continuously in one place.
**Hindi Meaning:** स्थिर रूप से रहना
**Synonyms:** Settle permanently
**Antonyms:** Peregrinate
**Example:** He chose to reside steadily in his hometown.

The word means constant travel. 'Wander extensively' is the closest synonym.

questions.text: Choose the antonym of 'Refulgent':
questions.solution:
Question word: **Refulgent**

**English Meaning:** Shining brilliantly or radiantly.
**Hindi Meaning:** अत्यंत चमकदार, तेजस्वी
**Synonyms:** Radiant, dazzling, luminous, brilliant
**Antonyms:** Dull, dim, obscure
**Example:** The refulgent success of the mission enhanced national pride.

**Option 0: radiant**
**English Meaning:** Shining brightly.
**Hindi Meaning:** चमकदार
**Synonyms:** Luminous
**Antonyms:** Dull
**Example:** Her radiant face showed joy.

**Option 1: dazzling**
**English Meaning:** Extremely bright.
**Hindi Meaning:** आँखें चौंधियाने वाला
**Synonyms:** Brilliant
**Antonyms:** Dull
**Example:** The dazzling lights lit the stage.

**Option 2: dull** (Correct)
**English Meaning:** Lacking brightness or shine.
**Hindi Meaning:** मंद, धुंधला
**Synonyms:** Dim, obscure
**Antonyms:** Refulgent
**Example:** The refulgent success of the mission enhanced national pride.

**Option 3: luminous**
**English Meaning:** Full of light.
**Hindi Meaning:** चमकीला
**Synonyms:** Radiant
**Antonyms:** Dull
**Example:** The luminous stars filled the sky.

**Option 4: brilliant**
**English Meaning:** Very bright and shining.
**Hindi Meaning:** तेजस्वी
**Synonyms:** Dazzling
**Antonyms:** Dull
**Example:** The brilliant diamond sparkled.

The question asks for the antonym. 'Dull' is the opposite of 'Refulgent'.

questions.text: Choose the word that best fits the blank: The argument became _____, offering repetition instead of fresh insights.
questions.solution:
Question word: **Tautologous** (the correct fit)

**English Meaning:** Repeating the same idea in different words; redundant.
**Hindi Meaning:** पुनरुक्ति वाला, अनावश्यक दोहराव
**Synonyms:** Repetitive, redundant, verbose
**Antonyms:** Concise, succinct, precise
**Example:** The argument became tautologous, offering repetition instead of fresh insights.

**Option 0: concise**
**English Meaning:** Brief and clear.
**Hindi Meaning:** संक्षिप्त
**Synonyms:** Succinct
**Antonyms:** Tautologous
**Example:** His concise reply saved time.

**Option 1: succinct**
**English Meaning:** Short and to the point.
**Hindi Meaning:** सारगर्भित
**Synonyms:** Precise
**Antonyms:** Tautologous
**Example:** The succinct report was appreciated.

**Option 2: tautologous** (Correct)
**English Meaning:** Repeating the same idea in different words; redundant.
**Hindi Meaning:** पुनरुक्ति वाला, अनावश्यक दोहराव
**Synonyms:** Repetitive, redundant
**Antonyms:** Concise, succinct
**Example:** The argument became tautologous, offering repetition instead of fresh insights.

**Option 3: precise**
**English Meaning:** Exact and accurate.
**Hindi Meaning:** सटीक
**Synonyms:** Succinct
**Antonyms:** Tautologous
**Example:** The precise answer impressed the panel.

**Option 4: brief**
**English Meaning:** Short in duration or length.
**Hindi Meaning:** संक्षिप्त
**Synonyms:** Concise
**Antonyms:** Tautologous
**Example:** The brief speech was effective.

The sentence shows unnecessary repetition. Only 'tautologous' fits.

questions.text: Choose the closest synonym for 'Uliginous':
questions.solution:
Question word: **Uliginous** (the correct synonym is 'marshy and boggy')

**English Meaning:** Muddy or marshy; full of wet, soft ground.
**Hindi Meaning:** दलदली, कीचड़युक्त
**Synonyms:** Marshy, swampy, boggy
**Antonyms:** Dry, arid, firm
**Example:** The uliginous terrain made infrastructure development extremely challenging.

**Option 0: dry and firm**
**English Meaning:** Without moisture and hard.
**Hindi Meaning:** सूखा और मजबूत
**Synonyms:** Arid
**Antonyms:** Uliginous
**Example:** The dry and firm soil supported heavy construction.

**Option 1: arid land**
**English Meaning:** Extremely dry area.
**Hindi Meaning:** शुष्क भूमि
**Synonyms:** Barren
**Antonyms:** Uliginous
**Example:** Farmers faced hardship in arid land.

**Option 2: marshy and boggy** (Correct)
**English Meaning:** Muddy or marshy; full of wet, soft ground.
**Hindi Meaning:** दलदली, कीचड़युक्त
**Synonyms:** Swampy
**Antonyms:** Dry, firm
**Example:** The uliginous terrain made infrastructure development extremely challenging.

**Option 3: solid ground**
**English Meaning:** Firm and stable earth.
**Hindi Meaning:** ठोस जमीन
**Synonyms:** Firm
**Antonyms:** Uliginous
**Example:** They built the house on solid ground.

**Option 4: barren terrain**
**English Meaning:** Empty and unproductive land.
**Hindi Meaning:** बंजर भूमि
**Synonyms:** Arid
**Antonyms:** Uliginous
**Example:** The barren terrain yielded no crops.

The word describes wet, soft land. 'Marshy and boggy' is the closest synonym.

questions.text: Choose the antonym of 'Virescent':
questions.solution:
Question word: **Virescent**

**English Meaning:** Becoming green or verdant; turning lush.
**Hindi Meaning:** हरा होता हुआ, हरितिमायुक्त
**Synonyms:** Greening, flourishing, verdant
**Antonyms:** Withering, barren, dry
**Example:** The virescent landscape after the monsoon reflected ecological renewal.

**Option 0: greening**
**English Meaning:** Becoming green.
**Hindi Meaning:** हरा होना
**Synonyms:** Verdant
**Antonyms:** Withering
**Example:** Spring brings greening to the hills.

**Option 1: flourishing**
**English Meaning:** Growing strongly.
**Hindi Meaning:** फल-फूल रहा
**Synonyms:** Lush
**Antonyms:** Withering
**Example:** The plants were flourishing.

**Option 2: withering** (Correct)
**English Meaning:** Drying up and dying.
**Hindi Meaning:** मुरझाना
**Synonyms:** Barren, dry
**Antonyms:** Virescent
**Example:** The virescent landscape after the monsoon reflected ecological renewal.

**Option 3: verdant**
**English Meaning:** Green and lush.
**Hindi Meaning:** हरा-भरा
**Synonyms:** Flourishing
**Antonyms:** Withering
**Example:** Verdant fields covered the valley.

**Option 4: lush**
**English Meaning:** Richly green.
**Hindi Meaning:** हरा-भरा
**Synonyms:** Verdant
**Antonyms:** Withering
**Example:** The lush garden looked beautiful.

The question asks for the antonym. 'Withering' is the opposite of 'Virescent'.

questions.text: Choose the word that best fits the blank: The foul odor from the waste site caused residents to _____ with discomfort.
questions.solution:
Question word: **Wamble** (the correct fit)

**English Meaning:** To feel nauseated or queasy.
**Hindi Meaning:** मिचली आना, जी मचलना
**Synonyms:** Nauseate, sicken, quease
**Antonyms:** Comfort, soothe, settle
**Example:** The foul odor from the waste site caused residents to wamble with discomfort.

**Option 0: comfort**
**English Meaning:** To make someone feel at ease.
**Hindi Meaning:** आराम देना
**Synonyms:** Soothe
**Antonyms:** Wamble
**Example:** The medicine helped comfort the patient.

**Option 1: soothe**
**English Meaning:** To calm or relieve.
**Hindi Meaning:** शांत करना
**Synonyms:** Comfort
**Antonyms:** Wamble
**Example:** The music soothed her nerves.

**Option 2: wamble** (Correct)
**English Meaning:** To feel nauseated or queasy.
**Hindi Meaning:** मिचली आना, जी मचलना
**Synonyms:** Sicken, quease
**Antonyms:** Comfort, soothe
**Example:** The foul odor from the waste site caused residents to wamble with discomfort.

**Option 3: settle**
**English Meaning:** To become calm.
**Hindi Meaning:** शांत होना
**Synonyms:** Relax
**Antonyms:** Wamble
**Example:** His stomach began to settle after the medicine.

**Option 4: relax**
**English Meaning:** To become less tense.
**Hindi Meaning:** आराम करना
**Synonyms:** Soothe
**Antonyms:** Wamble
**Example:** The hot tea helped him relax.

The sentence describes feeling sick. Only 'wamble' fits.

questions.text: Choose the closest synonym for 'Xerothermic':
questions.solution:
Question word: **Xerothermic** (the correct synonym is 'arid and dry')

**English Meaning:** Characterized by hot and dry conditions.
**Hindi Meaning:** गर्म और शुष्क, शुष्क-उष्ण
**Synonyms:** Arid, dry, desert-like
**Antonyms:** Humid, moist, temperate
**Example:** Xerothermic climates pose serious challenges for agriculture and water management.

**Option 0: humid and moist**
**English Meaning:** Full of moisture.
**Hindi Meaning:** नम और गीला
**Synonyms:** Wet
**Antonyms:** Xerothermic
**Example:** Humid and moist weather causes discomfort.

**Option 1: temperate climate**
**English Meaning:** Mild weather conditions.
**Hindi Meaning:** समशीतोष्ण जलवायु
**Synonyms:** Moderate
**Antonyms:** Xerothermic
**Example:** The temperate climate suits most crops.

**Option 2: arid and dry** (Correct)
**English Meaning:** Characterized by hot and dry conditions.
**Hindi Meaning:** गर्म और शुष्क, शुष्क-उष्ण
**Synonyms:** Desert-like
**Antonyms:** Humid, moist
**Example:** Xerothermic climates pose serious challenges for agriculture and water management.

**Option 3: rainy region**
**English Meaning:** Area with heavy rainfall.
**Hindi Meaning:** वर्षा वाला क्षेत्र
**Synonyms:** Wet
**Antonyms:** Xerothermic
**Example:** The rainy region supports lush forests.

**Option 4: cool and wet**
**English Meaning:** Low temperature with moisture.
**Hindi Meaning:** ठंडा और गीला
**Synonyms:** Humid
**Antonyms:** Xerothermic
**Example:** Cool and wet weather is common in hills.

The word describes hot-dry conditions. 'Arid and dry' is the closest synonym.

questions.text: Choose the antonym of 'Zephyrous':
questions.solution:
Question word: **Zephyrous**

**English Meaning:** Gentle like a soft breeze.
**Hindi Meaning:** मंद समीर जैसा, हल्की हवा वाला
**Synonyms:** Breezy, mild, gentle
**Antonyms:** Stormy, violent, harsh
**Example:** A zephyrous wind carried the fragrance of blooming flowers across the valley.

**Option 0: breezy**
**English Meaning:** Light and pleasant wind.
**Hindi Meaning:** हल्की हवा वाला
**Synonyms:** Gentle
**Antonyms:** Stormy
**Example:** The breezy morning felt refreshing.

**Option 1: mild**
**English Meaning:** Not harsh or severe.
**Hindi Meaning:** कोमल
**Synonyms:** Soft
**Antonyms:** Stormy
**Example:** The mild weather was perfect for travel.

**Option 2: stormy** (Correct)
**English Meaning:** With strong winds and rain.
**Hindi Meaning:** तूफानी
**Synonyms:** Violent, harsh
**Antonyms:** Zephyrous
**Example:** A zephyrous wind carried the fragrance of blooming flowers across the valley.

**Option 3: gentle**
**English Meaning:** Soft and calm.
**Hindi Meaning:** हल्का
**Synonyms:** Mild
**Antonyms:** Stormy
**Example:** The gentle breeze cooled the room.

**Option 4: soft**
**English Meaning:** Not strong or forceful.
**Hindi Meaning:** नरम
**Synonyms:** Gentle
**Antonyms:** Stormy
**Example:** The soft wind touched her face.

The question asks for the antonym. 'Stormy' is the opposite of 'Zephyrous'.

questions.text: Choose the word that best fits the blank: Prolonged _____ among officials often leads to a lack of empathy in public decision-making processes.
questions.solution:
Question word: **Aposiopathy** (the correct fit)

**English Meaning:** Suppression of emotion or passion.
**Hindi Meaning:** भावनाओं का दमन, मनोभावों का नियंत्रण
**Synonyms:** Repression, suppression, restraint
**Antonyms:** Expression, release, outpouring
**Example:** Prolonged aposiopathy among officials often leads to a lack of empathy in public decision-making processes.

**Option 0: expression**
**English Meaning:** Open display of feelings.
**Hindi Meaning:** अभिव्यक्ति
**Synonyms:** Outpouring
**Antonyms:** Aposiopathy
**Example:** Emotional expression helped the team.

**Option 1: release**
**English Meaning:** Freeing of emotions.
**Hindi Meaning:** मुक्ति
**Synonyms:** Venting
**Antonyms:** Aposiopathy
**Example:** The release of anger reduced stress.

**Option 2: aposiopathy** (Correct)
**English Meaning:** Suppression of emotion or passion.
**Hindi Meaning:** भावनाओं का दमन, मनोभावों का नियंत्रण
**Synonyms:** Repression, restraint
**Antonyms:** Expression, release
**Example:** Prolonged aposiopathy among officials often leads to a lack of empathy in public decision-making processes.

**Option 3: outpouring**
**English Meaning:** Sudden strong expression.
**Hindi Meaning:** उमड़ना
**Synonyms:** Release
**Antonyms:** Aposiopathy
**Example:** There was an outpouring of support.

**Option 4: venting**
**English Meaning:** Expressing pent-up feelings.
**Hindi Meaning:** निकालना
**Synonyms:** Expression
**Antonyms:** Aposiopathy
**Example:** Venting frustration helped him calm down.

The sentence shows emotional suppression. Only 'aposiopathy' fits.

questions.text: One-word substitute: Mercy shown by authority.
questions.solution:
Question word: **Clemency** (the correct fit)

**English Meaning:** Mercy shown by authority.
**Hindi Meaning:** दया, क्षमा
**Example:** The governor granted clemency to several non-violent offenders.

**Option 0: Decentralization**
**English Meaning:** Transfer of power from centre to local bodies.
**Hindi Meaning:** विकेंद्रीकरण
**Example:** Decentralization strengthened grassroots governance.

**Option 1: Consortium**
**English Meaning:** Association of organizations.
**Hindi Meaning:** संघ
**Example:** A global consortium funded the vaccine research.

**Option 2: Clemency** (Correct)
**English Meaning:** Mercy shown by authority.
**Hindi Meaning:** दया, क्षमा
**Example:** The governor granted clemency to several non-violent offenders.

**Option 3: Embargo**
**English Meaning:** Official ban on trade.
**Hindi Meaning:** प्रतिबंध
**Example:** The embargo severely affected export-dependent economies.

**Option 4: Amnesty**
**English Meaning:** General pardon for offenders.
**Hindi Meaning:** आम माफी
**Example:** The government declared amnesty for political prisoners.

The exact one-word substitute for 'Mercy shown by authority' is 'Clemency'.

**Option 4: Amalgamation**

**Example:** The amalgamation of banks improved capital strength.

The exact one-word substitute for 'Transfer of power from centre to local bodies' is 'Decentralization'.

**Example:** The embargo severely affected export-dependent economies.

**Hindi Meaning:** प्रत्यर्पण
**Example:** The suspect was returned under extradition treaty.

The exact one-word substitute for 'Official ban on trade' is 'Embargo'.

questions.text: Choose the correct meaning of the phrasal verb 'Turn up':
questions.solution:
Question word: **Turn up** (the correct meaning is 'Arrive or increase volume')

**English Meaning:** Arrive / increase volume.
**Hindi Meaning:** आना / आवाज बढ़ाना
**Example:** He turned up late for the meeting.

**Option 0: Reject or reduce**
**English Meaning:** Refuse or lower.
**Hindi Meaning:** अस्वीकार या कम करना
**Example:** She turned down the job offer.

**Option 1: Submit or go to bed**
**English Meaning:** Hand in or retire for sleep.
**Hindi Meaning:** जमा करना या सोने जाना
**Example:** He turned in his homework on time.

**Option 2: Arrive or increase volume** (Correct)
**English Meaning:** Arrive / increase volume.
**Hindi Meaning:** आना / आवाज बढ़ाना
**Example:** He turned up late for the meeting.

**Option 3: Result or attend**
**English Meaning:** Happen or be present.
**Hindi Meaning:** परिणाम या उपस्थित होना
**Example:** It turned out to be a success.

**Option 4: End or finish**
**English Meaning:** Conclude.
**Hindi Meaning:** समाप्त होना
**Example:** The meeting turned out late.

The phrasal verb 'Turn up' means to appear or make louder. The correct option is 'Arrive or increase volume'.

questions.text: Choose the correct meaning of the phrasal verb 'Turn out':
questions.solution:
Question word: **Turn out** (the correct meaning is 'Result or attend')

**English Meaning:** Result / attend.
**Hindi Meaning:** परिणाम निकलना / उपस्थित होना
**Example:** It turned out to be a success.

**Option 0: Arrive or increase volume**
**English Meaning:** Arrive / increase volume.
**Hindi Meaning:** आना / आवाज बढ़ाना
**Example:** He turned up late for the meeting.

**Option 1: Reject or reduce**
**English Meaning:** Refuse or lower.
**Hindi Meaning:** अस्वीकार या कम करना
**Example:** She turned down the job offer.

**Option 2: Submit or go to bed**
**English Meaning:** Hand in or retire for sleep.
**Hindi Meaning:** जमा करना या सोने जाना
**Example:** He turned in his homework.

**Option 3: Result or attend** (Correct)
**English Meaning:** Result / attend.
**Hindi Meaning:** परिणाम निकलना / उपस्थित होना
**Example:** It turned out to be a success.

**Option 4: End or finish**
**English Meaning:** Conclude.
**Hindi Meaning:** समाप्त होना
**Example:** The event turned out late.

The phrasal verb 'Turn out' means to prove or show up. The correct option is 'Result or attend'.

**Hindi Meaning:** समाप्त होना
**Example:** The meeting turned out late.

The phrasal verb 'Turn down' means to refuse or lower. The correct option is 'Reject or reduce'.

questions.text: Choose the correct meaning of the phrasal verb 'Turn in':
questions.solution:
Question word: **Turn in** (the correct meaning is 'Submit or go to bed')

**English Meaning:** Submit / go to bed.
**Hindi Meaning:** जमा करना या सोने जाना
**Example:** He turned in his homework on time.

**Option 0: Arrive or increase volume**
**English Meaning:** Arrive / increase volume.
**Hindi Meaning:** आना / आवाज बढ़ाना
**Example:** He turned up late for the meeting.

**Option 1: Result or attend**
**English Meaning:** Result / attend.
**Hindi Meaning:** परिणाम निकलना / उपस्थित होना
**Example:** It turned out to be a success.

**Option 2: Reject or reduce**
**English Meaning:** Reject / reduce.
**Hindi Meaning:** अस्वीकार या कम करना
**Example:** She turned down the job offer.

**Option 3: Submit or go to bed** (Correct)
**English Meaning:** Submit / go to bed.
**Hindi Meaning:** जमा करना या सोने जाना
**Example:** He turned in his homework on time.

**Option 4: End or finish**
**English Meaning:** Conclude.
**Hindi Meaning:** समाप्त होना
**Example:** The meeting turned out late.

The phrasal verb 'Turn in' means to hand over or retire. The correct option is 'Submit or go to bed'.

questions.text: Choose the correct meaning of the idiom 'A wolf in sheep's clothing':
options: A person who appears harmless but is actually dangerous
To have just enough money to survive
questions.solution:
Question word: **A wolf in sheep’s clothing** (the correct meaning is 'A person who appears harmless but is actually dangerous')

**English Meaning:** A person who appears harmless but is actually dangerous.
**Hindi Meaning:** भेड़ के चमड़े में भेड़िया
**Example:** The charming salesman turned out to be a wolf in sheep’s clothing.

**Option 0: To raise a false alarm**
**English Meaning:** To raise a false alarm.
**Hindi Meaning:** झूठी चेतावनी देना
**Example:** He kept crying wolf.

**Option 1: To eat something very quickly**
**English Meaning:** To eat something very quickly.
**Hindi Meaning:** जल्दी खाना
**Example:** He wolfed down his lunch.

**Option 2: A person who appears harmless but is actually dangerous** (Correct)
**English Meaning:** A person who appears harmless but is actually dangerous.
**Hindi Meaning:** भेड़ के चमड़े में भेड़िया
**Example:** The charming salesman turned out to be a wolf in sheep’s clothing.

**Option 3: To abandon someone without support**
**English Meaning:** To abandon someone without support.
**Hindi Meaning:** किसी को मुसीबत में अकेला छोड़ना
**Example:** The manager threw the intern to the wolves.

**Option 4: To have just enough money to survive**
**English Meaning:** To have just enough money to survive.
**Hindi Meaning:** भूख से बचने लायक पैसा
**Example:** The salary helped keep the wolf from the door.

The idiom describes a dangerous person in disguise. The correct option is 'A person who appears harmless but is actually dangerous'.

questions.text: Choose the correct meaning of the idiom 'Cry wolf':
options: A person who appears harmless but is dangerous
To have just enough money to survive
questions.solution:
Question word: **Cry wolf** (the correct meaning is 'To raise a false alarm')

**English Meaning:** To raise a false alarm.
**Hindi Meaning:** झूठी चेतावनी देना
**Example:** He kept crying wolf, so no one believed him when the real danger came.

**Option 0: A person who appears harmless but is dangerous**
**English Meaning:** A person who appears harmless but is dangerous.
**Hindi Meaning:** भेड़ के चमड़े में भेड़िया
**Example:** The salesman was a wolf in sheep’s clothing.

**Option 1: To eat something very quickly**
**English Meaning:** To eat something very quickly.
**Hindi Meaning:** जल्दी खाना
**Example:** He wolfed down his lunch.

**Option 2: To raise a false alarm** (Correct)
**English Meaning:** To raise a false alarm.
**Hindi Meaning:** झूठी चेतावनी देना
**Example:** He kept crying wolf, so no one believed him when the real danger came.

**Option 3: To abandon someone without support**
**English Meaning:** To abandon someone without support.
**Hindi Meaning:** किसी को मुसीबत में अकेला छोड़ना
**Example:** The manager threw the intern to the wolves.

**Option 4: To have just enough money to survive**
**English Meaning:** To have just enough money to survive.
**Hindi Meaning:** भूख से बचने लायक पैसा
**Example:** The salary helped keep the wolf from the door.

The idiom means giving false warnings. The correct option is 'To raise a false alarm'.
